body.question: What is FIMBY?
body.answer: FIMBY (Family In My Backyard) is a private neighbourhood platform that helps you connect with the people who live closest to you. Share what's happening through Shared Life, post asks and offers, borrow and lend through the neighbourhood library, message neighbours directly, and look out for one another. Only registered neighbours in your neighbourhood can see your activity.

body.question: How do I get started?
body.answer: The quickest way to find your way around is the tour - tap Take the Tour at the top of this Help page any time. To move around the app, use the bar along the bottom: Home, Library, Messages, and My Stuff. The + button in the middle is how you create a new post. The menu in the top-right corner holds your Profile, Settings, and Help, and the bell shows your notifications.

body.question: How do I update my profile?
body.answer: Tap the menu in the top-right corner and choose Profile. From there you can edit your name, photo, pronouns, bio, and care preferences. You can update your profile any time.

body.question: What is my neighbourhood, and who can see what I share?
body.answer: FIMBY is organised by neighbourhood, and your neighbourhood is your trust boundary. Your profile, posts, stories, and library items are visible only to other registered neighbours in your neighbourhood - never to the public or to people outside it. Your contact details stay private until you choose to share them.

body.question: What are Asks and Offers?
body.answer: Asks and Offers is your neighbourhood bulletin board. Post something you need (an ask) or something you would like to share (an offer) - goods, a helping hand, or an invitation. To create one, tap the + button in the bottom bar and choose Make an Ask or Make an Offer. Browse what neighbours have posted from the Home feed and respond to anything that interests you.

body.question: What is a Bulk Buy?
body.answer: A Bulk Buy is a shared purchase that a neighbour coordinates - buying something in bulk and splitting it into shares. To start one, tap the + button and choose Bulk Buy. To join one, open the bulk buy and tap Reserve A Share; the organiser will confirm pickup details. FIMBY does not handle payments - money and pickup are arranged between neighbours.

body.question: What are Events, and what's the difference between the types?
body.answer:
Events are posts for things happening in your neighbourhood. There are three kinds:

  • Gathering - a smaller hosted get-together with limited spots. You RSVP, and the host accepts guests until it is full.
  • Open Event - everyone is welcome and there is no limit. Tap I'm Going to let the host know.
  • Community Event - something a neighbour is sharing that someone else is running. Tap I'm Interested. It is a heads-up, not a commitment.

To post an event, tap the + button and choose Post an Event.

body.question: How do I RSVP or join an event?
body.answer: Open the event and tap its button. For a Gathering, tap RSVP. For an Open Event, tap I'm Going. For a Community Event, tap I'm Interested. Only Gatherings have limited spots - Open and Community Events are always open and never fill up. You can change your mind any time by tapping the same button again.

body.question: What is Shared Life?
body.answer:
Shared Life is where neighbours share what's happening in their lives. Tap the + button and choose Share in Shared Life, then pick the kind of post:

  • Thank You - express gratitude to someone
  • God Story - share a faith experience
  • Prayer Request - ask for prayer and support
  • Introduction - introduce yourself to neighbours
  • Support Needed - share a struggle and seek comfort
  • Neighbourhood Moment - share a moment you noticed

Shared Life posts appear in the Home feed for neighbours to read and respond to.

body.question: How do I comment on or respond to a post?
body.answer: It depends on the post. On a Shared Life post, tap the comment icon to add a comment - you can mention a neighbour with an @ symbol. On an ask, offer, or event, tap its button to respond or RSVP. Once you respond, a private thread opens where you and the other neighbour can sort out the details.

body.question: How do I edit or delete something I posted?
body.answer: Open your post or Shared Life story and look for the menu in the page header - it shows Edit, Photo, and Delete as buttons on a wide screen, or under the three-dot menu on a phone. Edit changes the details, Photo adds or swaps an image, and Delete removes it. These options only appear on content you created.

body.question: How does the Library work?
body.answer: The Library is your neighbourhood's shared shelf. Open the Library tab to browse what neighbours are sharing - filter by All, Items, or Skills, and narrow by category. Borrow what you need and lend what you have. To add your own, tap Lend an Item or Offer a Skill. Borrowing from the library needs a vouch from a neighbour first - see vouching below.

body.question: How do I borrow an item?
body.answer: Open the item and tap Borrow, then Submit Request. The owner reviews it and, once they approve, shares pickup details with you. When you have collected it, tap I Have the Item. You can use Message Owner any time to coordinate. When you are done, tap Return Item.

body.question: How do I lend my things and approve requests?
body.answer: Add something with Lend an Item from the Library. When a neighbour asks to borrow it, tap Review to see the request, then Approve & Share to approve and share pickup details. When they collect it, tap Mark as Picked Up, and when it comes back, tap Verify Return & Condition. You can also set an item to auto-accept requests if you would rather not review each one.

body.question: How do I return an item or ask for more time?
body.answer: To return something you borrowed, open the item (or its message thread) and tap Return Item, then confirm. If you need it longer, tap Request Extension and suggest a new date - the owner can approve or decline. Owners confirm returns with Verify Return & Condition.

body.question: How do I message a neighbour?
body.answer: Open the Messages tab and tap New, then search for a neighbour and pick them to start a conversation. You can also message someone from their profile or from a post they made. All your conversations live in the Messages tab.

body.question: Can I start a group chat?
body.answer: Group chats are available for Open Events. If you are hosting an Open Event, open it and tap Create Event Chat to start a group conversation with everyone who is going. For everyone else, one-to-one messages work great - just start a conversation from the Messages tab.

body.question: How do I thank someone?
body.answer: When a neighbour helps you through an ask, offer, or a lend, open the response and tap Say Thanks, then write a note and tap Send Thanks. Your thank-you appears as a little celebration right inside that conversation, and the neighbour gets a notification.

body.question: What is vouching, and why does it matter?
body.answer: Vouching is how neighbours welcome one another into the trusted circle. A vouch is personal - one neighbour, or a community group representative, saying they know you and you are good. A vouch is what opens the lending library for you. To ask for one, tap Request a vouch (you will see this when you visit the library before being vouched, or from your profile), choose a neighbour or community group, and send the request. You need to be acting as yourself to give or request a vouch.

body.question: How do I report content or a concern?
body.answer: If something does not feel right, open the three-dot menu on the post or message and choose Report. Your report is private and reviewed by your neighbourhood team, usually within 24 hours. For anything urgent or safety-related, use the Email Safety Team button on this page. Reporting is separate from blocking.

body.question: How do I block someone?
body.answer: You can block a neighbour from their profile - open the three-dot menu and choose Block - or from Settings under Blocked Contacts by tapping Block someone. Blocking is mutual: neither of you will see each other's posts, messages, or profile. You can unblock from the same Blocked Contacts list any time.

body.question: Who is my neighbourhood moderator?
body.answer: Your moderator is a neighbour who helps keep your neighbourhood safe and welcoming. If your neighbourhood has one, you will see them on this Help page under Your Neighbourhood Moderator, with a Message Your Moderator button so you can reach out about anything affecting the community.

body.question: What are the community guidelines?
body.answer: The community guidelines explain how we treat each other on FIMBY, what is not allowed, and how reports are handled. You can read them any time from the Community Guidelines & Safety section on this Help page.

body.question: Can I post or respond on behalf of someone else?
body.answer: Yes, if you support a neighbour or represent a community group. Once a support connection is approved, you can switch into that person's or group's identity and act for them. When you do, FIMBY shows a green Posting as banner so it is always clear who you are acting for. To set this up, open Manage identities from the top-right menu and tap Set Up Connection, then choose Support a neighbour or Represent a community group. A neighbourhood moderator reviews the request. Note that vouching always has to be done as yourself.

body.question: How do I switch who I'm acting as?
body.answer: Open the menu in the top-right corner. Under your identities you will see yourself and anyone you support or represent - tap one to switch into it. While you are acting as someone else, a small chip appears next to the FIMBY logo; tap it any time to switch back to yourself. You manage all of this from Manage identities.

body.question: What is a community group profile?
body.answer: A community group profile is a page for a local organisation - a church, charity, or group - that neighbours can view and message. If you represent an approved group, you can switch into it and edit its profile: logo, description, website, and contact details. Approved groups show a verified badge.

body.question: How do I share my contact info with a neighbour?
body.answer: Your contact details stay private until you choose to share them. When you are in a response or conversation with a neighbour, use Share Contact Info and tick exactly what you want to share - Email, Phone, or Address. You will see a preview before it is sent. You can also turn on Auto-Share Contact Info when you create an ask or offer so it is shared automatically with whoever responds.

body.question: How do I change my notification settings?
body.answer: Open Settings from the top-right menu and go to Notifications and Email. You can turn Push Notifications and Email Alerts on or off, set Quiet Hours so alerts wait until a quiet window ends, choose which kinds of updates reach you by push or email, and pick how often you get the Neighbourhood Digest. Important account and safety messages always come through.

body.question: What are care preferences?
body.answer: Care preferences are an optional way to let neighbours know how best to support you. You can set them during onboarding and edit them any time from your Profile. They are always optional, and you choose what to share.

body.question: How do I delete or deactivate my account?
body.answer: Open Settings from the top-right menu and scroll to Delete account. Deleting deactivates your login right away. Your data is kept for 30 days so you can restore your account if you change your mind, then it is permanently removed. You can also choose to skip the 30-day window and delete immediately.
